title_en: Kitab al-Salah: Prayer — Conditions and Pillars
content_en:
Prayer (salah) occupies the central position in Islamic worship, and al-Shafi'i devotes the most extensive section of al-Umm to its rules. He distinguishes sharply between the conditions (shurut) that must be met before prayer begins and the pillars (arkan) that constitute the prayer itself, arguing that the absence of any pillar invalidates the prayer entirely, while the absence of a condition prevents the prayer from being legally valid in the first place.

The conditions for valid prayer include: ritual purity (taharah) from both minor and major impurity, physical cleanliness of body, clothing, and place, facing the qiblah (direction of the Ka'bah), covering the awrah (the obligatory area of the body that must be concealed), entering the proper prayer time, and making the intention (niyyah). Al-Shafi'i insists that the intention must be made in the heart at the moment of the opening takbir; verbalizing it aloud is recommended by many later Shafi'i scholars as an aid to focusing the heart, though the verbal utterance itself is not the operative act.

The Shafi'i school enumerates fourteen pillars of the obligatory prayer. These are: the standing position (qiyam) when able, the opening takbir (Allahu Akbar), recitation of Surah al-Fatiha in every rak'ah, bowing (ruku') with tranquility (tuma'ninah), rising from the bow, prostration (sujud) on seven body parts with tranquility, sitting between the two prostrations, the final tashahhud, sitting for it, invoking blessings on the Prophet (salawat) in the final tashahhud, the final salam to the right, and maintaining the sequence of all these acts (tartib).

One of the most distinctive Shafi'i rulings in the chapter on prayer is the obligation to recite Surah al-Fatiha in every rak'ah, including those in which the imam recites aloud. Al-Shafi'i cites the famous hadith: 'There is no prayer for the one who does not recite the Opening of the Book,' as establishing Fatiha's obligatory status for every worshipper. This directly conflicts with the Hanafi position, which holds that the follower's recitation is subsumed under the imam's, and with the Maliki position that reciting behind the imam in the loud prayers is discouraged.

Another well-known Shafi'i position concerns the Bismillah at the start of Fatiha. Al-Shafi'i holds that Bismillah is the first verse of Surah al-Fatiha and must therefore be recited aloud in prayers where Fatiha is recited aloud. This makes the Shafi'i prayer immediately distinguishable from the Hanafi and Maliki prayers, where Bismillah is either recited silently or not considered part of Fatiha at all. Al-Shafi'i's argument rests on the placement of Bismillah in the written Quran and the practice of certain Companions.

On the qunut supplication in Fajr prayer, al-Shafi'i holds it to be a confirmed sunnah recited in the second rak'ah of Fajr after rising from the bow. This practice is not observed in the Hanafi or Maliki schools, though the Hanbali school restricts qunut to times of communal affliction. Al-Shafi'i's position rests on the practice of Anas ibn Malik and other Companions who reportedly continued the Fajr qunut throughout their lives.

For the prayer of the sick, traveler, and person in fear, al-Shafi'i provides detailed rulings on shortening (qasr) and combining (jam') prayers, the conditions under which each is permitted, and what portions of the prayer's form may be modified without invalidating it. Throughout, the text reflects al-Shafi'i's commitment to grounding every ruling in specific hadith evidence.
title_id: Kitab al-Shalah: Shalat — Syarat-syarat dan Rukun-rukunnya
content_id:
Shalat menduduki posisi sentral dalam ibadah Islam, dan al-Syafi'i mendedikasikan bagian terluas dari Al-Umm untuk membahas hukum-hukumnya. Beliau membedakan dengan tegas antara syarat-syarat (syuruth) yang harus terpenuhi agar shalat sah dan rukun-rukun (arkan) yang merupakan komponen shalat itu sendiri, keduanya berdasarkan dalil-dalil yang kuat.

Pembahasan mencakup: waktu-waktu shalat wajib dan sunnah, adzan dan iqamat, arah kiblat, menutup aurat, thaharah sebagai syarat, niat, takbiratul ihram, bacaan Al-Fatihah (wajib dalam setiap rakaat menurut al-Syafi'i), rukuk dan sujud, tasyahhud, salam, sujud sahwi, shalat berjamaah dan imamah, shalat Jumat, shalat qasar dan jamak bagi musafir, shalat dalam keadaan sakit, shalat jenazah, shalat sunnah berbagai jenis, dan khilaf dengan Imam Malik dan Abu Hanifah beserta dalil-dalilnya.
content_ar:
تحتل الصلاة مكانة المحور في العبادة الإسلامية، ويُخصّص الشافعي الجزء الأوسع من الأم لأحكامها. ويُفرّق تفريقاً حاداً بين الشروط التي تتوقف عليها صحة الصلاة وبين الأركان التي تتألف منها الصلاة ذاتها.

والشروط التي يُفصّلها الشافعي: الطهارة من الحدثين، وستر العورة، واستقبال القبلة، ودخول الوقت، والنية. وفي كل شرط يستند إلى النصوص الواردة فيه، ويُبيّن الحالات التي يُعذر فيها عن استيفاء الشرط كالمريض والمسافر.

وأركان الصلاة عند الشافعي: النية وتكبيرة الإحرام والقيام للقادر وقراءة الفاتحة في كل ركعة، ذاهباً إلى أن من لم يقرأ الفاتحة لا صلاة له، وهو موقف تميّز به عن الحنفية. ويستدل على ذلك بحديث «لا صلاة لمن لم يقرأ بأم الكتاب».
